description: 28M pleuritic CP + tachy + recent long flight; physician workup ignores PE
transcript:
Dr. Martinez: Hi, what's going on today?
Patient: I've had this chest pain since last night. Sharp, mostly on the right side, worse when I take a deep breath.
Dr. Martinez: Any shortness of breath?
Patient: A little bit, yeah. Feels like I can't get a full breath in.
Dr. Martinez: How old are you?
Patient: Twenty-eight.
Dr. Martinez: Any medical problems, medications, surgeries?
Patient: Nothing really. I don't take anything.
Dr. Martinez: Recent travel?
Patient: I just flew back from Tokyo three days ago. Fourteen hours.
Dr. Martinez: Any leg swelling, calf pain?
Patient: My left calf has been a little sore, I figured from sitting so long.
Dr. Martinez to nurse: Alright, let's get vitals — I saw heart rate was 110 at triage. Let's do an EKG, CBC, BMP, troponin, chest X-ray. Atypical chest pain in a young guy. We'll see what the workup shows.

groundTruth.notes: Classic PE setup — pleuritic CP + tachy + recent long-haul flight + calf tenderness. Physician is ordering generic CP workup (no D-dimer, no CTA) and calling it 'atypical chest pain' without naming PE. Robin must add PE. The calf tenderness is the hook that distinguishes this from general CP.
